title: Third-Person Project Setup

video.note: Students build a castle environment in Unreal Engine while recording daily screen/camera evidence for a making-of documentary.

learningTarget: I can set up a playable third-person Unreal project and document why player scale matters for a castle walkthrough.

bellRinger.prompt: Why might a castle look different when viewed from a player camera instead of the editor camera?

vocabulary.definition: A dated record of what was built, what was captured, what changed, and what needs to happen next.
Video captured from the computer screen to show workflow, testing, or before/after changes.
Camera or screen-recorded evidence that shows how a project was made.
Supporting footage that helps explain a process, place, detail, or action.
The Q3 Unreal scene students build, refine, walk through, and document.

assignment.title: Third-Person Walkthrough Setup
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the playable character in the project.
Short note explaining why player scale matters.
Daily screen recording of a player-view test.

exitTicket: What did your recording show about player scale or movement?
